Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows mild hypoglycemia and low HbA1c, suggesting possible chronic low glucose. Elevated creatinine indicates impaired renal function. Urinalysis reveals significant proteinuria, ketonuria, glucosuria, and abnormal urine appearance, suggesting possible nephropathy and metabolic disturbance. No evidence of urinary infection. Recommend further evaluation for renal disease, glycemic control, and assessment for underlying metabolic or systemic conditions.
